summary: Improved matching in the lbSnap library code by replacing placeholder-style implementations/signatures with more structured C, extracting small inline helpers, and reducing lbsnap's unmatched surface to two functions. The PR was closed without merge in the provided context, but decomp.dev reported five new 100% matches and one additional improvement in lbSnap_8001DA5C.

key_files.role: Main matching work for lbSnap functions, helper extraction, RGB/tiled-offset cleanup, return-flow reshaping, and save-data offset factoring.

what_changed: Added static inline lbSnap_ClearText(char* text) to replace repeated loops clearing 0x21-byte local text buffers in lbSnap_8001D5FC, lbSnap_8001D7B0, and lbSnap_8001E058.

Reshaped several card-result functions to cache ptr->card_result in a local ret, only call the memory-card operation when ret is zero, and return ret at the end instead of using early return/else patterns.
Refactored lbSnap_8001DA5C from register-like temporaries into named variables such as dst, dst_x, src_x, dst_col, tile_column, dst_y, src_y_accum, and pixel.
Added static inline tiled image offset helpers lbSnap_GetTiledRGBOffset and lbSnap_GetTiledYOff for RGB/tiled pixel addressing.
Kept the RGB565_TO_RGB5A3 conversion macro and made its use clearer inside lbSnap_8001DA5C while improving that function from 47.05% to 55.88% according to the bot report.
Changed lbSnap_8001DC0C to use u32 seconds and call OSTicksToCalendarTime(OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds), &time) directly, which brought the function to 100% in the bot report.
Added static inline lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset(struct Unk80433380_0* snap) to factor the previously commented offset expression used for lbSnap_803BACC8.x14.
Reworked lbSnap_8001DF20 to use a local snap pointer and lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset; the PR notes this function remained at 98.15790% due to register allocation only.
Reworked lbSnap_8001DF6C to use a local descriptor pointer desc, a chan_arg local, lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset, and desc field references; decomp.dev reported this function improved from 86.41% to 100%.
Updated the public header declaration for lbSnap_8001E27C from UNK_RET lbSnap_8001E27C(UNK_PARAMS) to void lbSnap_8001E27C(void).
smart_moves: Extracting lbSnap_ClearText turned repeated manual zeroing loops into an inline helper while preserving the exact fixed 0x21-byte behavior needed for matching.
Using a ret local for card_result paths avoided duplicated returns and matched codegen for several nearly identical functions.
Factoring the save-data offset expression into lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset documented the apparent offsetof-style computation without relying on an unsafe or unproven struct annotation in the diff.
Replacing register-named temporaries in lbSnap_8001DA5C with semantic image-coordinate names made the remaining unmatched function easier to reason about without pretending it was fully solved.
The tiled-offset helpers separated address arithmetic from conversion logic, making the RGB565-to-RGB5A3 icon-generation path more searchable and reusable.
The author included concrete verification evidence: configure plus ninja, 20/22 lbsnap functions at 100%, and the two remaining blockers with percentages.
decomp_lessons: For repeated stack text buffers, a small static inline helper can improve both readability and matching if it preserves the exact loop bounds and call placement.
When multiple functions share card_result/stateChanged gating logic, matching may depend on assigning the result to a local before the conditional and returning that local after the operation.

For image/tile conversion functions, replacing register placeholders with domain names such as src_x, dst_y, tile_column, and src_y_accum can expose the original algorithm and make further matching easier.
Offset expressions that look like offsetof plus a runtime base field can be wrapped in an inline helper to reduce duplication while keeping uncertain struct semantics explicit.
Small type changes around OS time conversion can matter for matching; here OSTime seconds became u32 seconds with an explicit u64 cast into OSSecondsToTicks.
A partially matched large loop can still be valuable if it improves decompilation percentage and gives future contributors better variable names and helper boundaries.
naming_conventions: New helper names use the project symbol prefix and Pascal-style component after it: lbSnap_ClearText, lbSnap_GetTiledRGBOffset, lbSnap_GetTiledYOff, lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset.
Function symbols retain address-based decomp names such as lbSnap_8001D5FC, lbSnap_8001DA5C, lbSnap_8001DF20, and lbSnap_8001E058.

Locals in refactored code moved away from register-like names r4/r5/r6/r7/r9/r10/r25/r27/r28 toward semantic names like dst, dst_x, src_x, dst_col, tile_column, dst_y, src_y_accum, pixel, snap, desc, and chan_arg.
Header cleanup replaced placeholder signature macros UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with an exact void lbSnap_8001E27C(void) declaration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce static inline helpers rather than out-of-line functions when abstracting repeated logic needed for matching.
Cache global or struct field reads into locals to influence register allocation and return-flow shape.
Use final-return ret patterns instead of early returns when the original codegen appears to preserve a result register/local through the branch.

Use PAD_STACK only where needed for stack-shape matching; lbSnap_8001DF6C removed a PAD_STACK(8) while other functions retained PAD_STACK(8), and lbSnap_8001DA5C changed from PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(8).
Convert pointer arithmetic to byte-pointer indexed loads/stores, e.g. u8* plus helper-computed offsets, while casting at the final u16 access.
For tiled texture/icon data, isolate formulas involving x / 4, y / 4, x % 4, y % 4, tile stride, shifts by 5, and 2-byte pixel offsets.
Use explicit casts around OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds) when matching time conversion code.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased by 0.06% / 2424 bytes for GALE01 c59a5c6 - 3dd9ec7.
New 100% matches reported for lbSnap_8001DF6C, lbSnap_8001D7B0, lbSnap_8001D5FC, lbSnap_8001E058, and lbSnap_8001DC0C.
lbSnap_8001DA5C improved from 47.05% to 55.88% but remained unmatched.
Verification listed as python configure.py && ninja, with main/melee/lb/lbsnap at 20/22 functions at 100%.
Remaining blockers were lbSnap_8001DA5C at 55.87963% and lbSnap_8001DF20 at 98.15790%, with lbSnap_8001DF20 described as register allocation only.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching lbSnap_8001DA5C or reuse lbSnap_GetTiledRGBOffset/lbSnap_GetTiledYOff.
Find later PRs that resolve lbSnap_8001DF20 register allocation after lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset was introduced.
Search for other lbsnap functions using card_result ret-local patterns to compare matching tactics.
Search for additional header cleanup replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete void signatures.
